actionable_feedback: Add a prioritized, concrete mitigation/next-steps section that specifies exact follow-up experiments when no run meets the rule (see planned examples below). Each recommendation should include intended change, quantitative expectation, number of replicates, and acceptance criteria.

For the near-miss Run2 (impurity = 10%): recommend an immediate action (e.g., replicate Run2 n=3 under identical conditions and/or Run2 with a specified purification or quench change). Specify analytical method (e.g., HPLC method ID, sample prep), expected impurity reduction (e.g., target <= 9%), and decision criteria for acceptance.

Provide at least two mechanistic follow-ups to resolve the impurity boundary (examples: 1) test solvent swap: EtOAc -> MeCN at 80C with 3 mol% catalyst; 2) test purification/workup modification: change quench and include a specified chromatographic/purification step). For each, list exact parameters (temp, catalyst mol%, solvent, workup), number of replicates, and expected magnitude of change in impurity.

Add a short reproducibility checklist and machine-readable supporting files: include decision_table.csv with the three runs, calculations_appendix.md with formulas and a checksum, and an example command or tiny script to recompute the margins.

Add explicit units and, if available, assay uncertainty or recommended number of replicates to assess analytical variability. If you propose relaxing the decision boundary (e.g., impurity <= 10), justify with experimental plan and acceptance criteria.

Document the immediate recommended next single action (short, one-line instruction) so lab staff can act without reading the whole note (e.g., 'Run: replicate Run2 x3; if impurity <= 9% in majority, accept; otherwise run purification test A').
artifact_specific_comments: The artifact correctly calculates yield and impurity margins and explicitly flags Run2 as a boundary case (impurity = 10%).

Missing: concrete experimental mitigations (e.g., replicate counts, purification approach, exact parameter adjustments) and an explicit prioritized next-step recommendation anchored to acceptance criteria.
Include machine-readable files (CSV) and a checksum to aid downstream automated verification.
